title: Abstract (English)
part: abstracts in Hebrew, English, and layman's terms
generation_instructions: Write a concise abstract in English that includes: 1. Background and aim of the research. 2. A summary of the methods used. 3. Expected results. 4. The importance of the research to medicine. 5. Five relevant keywords. Ensure the abstract is easily understandable and highlights the key aspects of the research.
search_queries: example of a good research abstract
how to write a compelling abstract

title: Abstract (Hebrew)
part: abstracts in Hebrew, English, and layman's terms
generation_instructions: Write a concise abstract in Hebrew that includes: 1. Background and aim of the research. 2. A summary of the methods used. 3. Expected results. 4. The importance of the research to medicine. 5. Five relevant keywords. Ensure the abstract is accurate and culturally appropriate for a Hebrew-speaking audience.
search_queries: example of a good research abstract in Hebrew
how to write a compelling abstract in Hebrew
translation of scientific abstracts to Hebrew

title: Abstract (Layman's term)
part: abstracts in Hebrew, English, and layman's terms
generation_instructions: Write a concise abstract in layman's terms that includes: 1. Background and aim of the research, explained simply. 2. A summary of the methods used, explained simply. 3. Expected results. 4. The importance of the research to medicine, explained simply. 5. Five relevant keywords. Focus on clarity and avoid technical jargon.
search_queries: example of a good abstract in layman's terms
how to write a research abstract for the public

title: Scientific Background
generation_instructions: Provide a comprehensive scientific background that: 1. Reviews relevant literature, highlighting key findings and methodologies. 2. Identifies gaps in current knowledge and inconsistencies in previous research. 3. Clearly states the problem the research addresses and its significance. 4. Justifies the need for the proposed research, emphasizing its potential impact on the field. 5. Conclude by summarizing the rationale for the proposed study and its potential contribution to the existing body of knowledge.
search_queries: how to write a strong scientific background
writing a compelling rationale for research

title: Research Question
generation_instructions: Clearly state the research question, including: 1. The main hypothesis to be tested, ensuring it is testable and falsifiable. 2. Specific aims and objectives of the research, outlining what you intend to achieve. 3. Expected outcomes and their potential impact on the field, highlighting the significance of the research. Ensure the research question aligns with the identified knowledge gaps and builds upon the scientific background.
search_queries: how to formulate a clear research question
defining research aims and objectives effectively
examples of strong and impactful research questions
aligning research questions with knowledge gaps

title: Study Design
generation_instructions: Describe the study design, including: 1. The experimental design and rationale, justifying the chosen approach. 2. Characteristics of the study population, including inclusion and exclusion criteria. 3. Methods for data collection, detailing the procedures and instruments used. 4. Identification of key variables, both independent and dependent. 5. Control measures to ensure validity and minimize bias. Explain how the study design will effectively address the research question and test the hypothesis.
search_queries: types of experimental designs and their applications
data collection methods in scientific research
identifying and controlling variables in research
ensuring validity and reliability in study design

title: Detailed Plan
generation_instructions: Provide a detailed plan of the research, including: 1. Step-by-step procedures for each experiment, ensuring clarity and reproducibility. 2. A timeline with specific milestones, outlining key stages of the research. 3. Methods for data analysis and interpretation, specifying the statistical techniques to be used. Include a contingency plan to address potential challenges and ensure the project stays on track.
search_queries: writing detailed and reproducible experimental protocols
creating effective research timelines and milestones
data analysis techniques for scientific research
project management strategies for research projects

title: Methods (including sample size justification and available equipment)
generation_instructions: Describe the methods used in the research, including: 1. Justification for the sample size, explaining how it was determined and its adequacy. 2. Statistical power analysis, demonstrating the study's ability to detect meaningful effects. 3. Available equipment and resources, detailing their specifications and suitability for the research. 4. Specific techniques for data collection and analysis, ensuring rigor and accuracy. Clearly explain the rationale behind each methodological choice and its impact on the study's validity.
search_queries: sample size justification methods in research
equipment and resources for scientific research
data analysis techniques and their applications

title: Preliminary Results
generation_instructions: Present preliminary results, including: 1. Pilot data supporting the feasibility of the research, demonstrating its viability. 2. Proof-of-concept experiments, validating the underlying principles. 3. Early findings that validate the research approach, providing initial evidence of its effectiveness. Clearly articulate the significance of these preliminary results and their implications for the proposed research.
search_queries: validating research approaches with early findings

title: Schematic Representation with Responsibilities and Time Scheme
generation_instructions: Provide a schematic representation of the research plan, including: 1. A flowchart of the experimental process, illustrating the workflow. 2. A timeline for each stage of the research, outlining key deadlines. 3. Assignment of responsibilities to team members, ensuring accountability. Clearly depict the project's organization and the roles of each participant.
search_queries: project management timelines and their construction
task assignment strategies in research teams

title: Time Estimate for each Stage
generation_instructions: Provide a time estimate for each stage of the research, including: 1. A detailed timeline with specific milestones, outlining key deliverables. 2. Estimated duration for each task, ensuring realistic scheduling. 3. Scheduling considerations for project management, addressing potential delays. Justify the time estimates based on prior experience and available resources.
